name: CLIR-Bench: Benchmarking Multimodal Question Answering over Irregular Clinical Time Series
summary: arXiv:2607.09880v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Clinical time series are central to patient monitoring, risk assessment, and clinical decision support. However, they are often sparse, irregularly...
description: arXiv:2607.09880v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Clinical time series are central to patient monitoring, risk assessment, and clinical decision support. However, they are often sparse, irregularly sampled, and asynchronous, making it difficult for models to identify the temporal evidence required for clinical Question Answering (QA). Existing benchmarks primarily focus on regularly sampled tim...
author: Frank Nie, Ethan B. Liu, Yuan Zhu, Loe Yan, Wei Fan, Jindong Han
